tweets.text:
NBA simulation stats time:

In the 1999 NBA Finals, the Knicks vs. Spurs went 5 games in this order:

Game 1: Spurs 
Game 2: Spurs 
Game 3: Knicks
Game 4: Spurs
Game 5: Spurs

Can the Knicks pull off the same 5 game pattern tonight?

Game 1: Knicks
Game 2: Knicks
Game 3: Spurs
Game 4: Knicks
Game 5: Knicks? (Tonight)

Also the last time they won a championship in 1973, the series went 5 games too with Knicks winning 4-1. 

Simulation much?!

PS: The $SPX in 1973 traded at just 91. It’s been that long ago. Heh.

Canada saved their World Cup chances with a last minute goal, tying the game at full time, resulting in a Draw. 

Not one of the 1,250 entries managed to guess all 6/6 markets correctly, and only 3 managed to get 5/6 right. 

The Canada leaderboard now looks like this 👇 https://t.co/MeVQPQB4B1

The USA team came out and completely dominated Paraguay, going up 3 goals at halftime.

Interestingly, we had 21 entrants (!) all get 5/6 correct and they all seemed to miss getting the final score of 4:1 (I see you @alexjmingolla)

How far is this USA team going to go?? https://t.co/CNDiX0TREV
